title: Angular Web Architecture — NXGN Actions
purpose: **AI Summary**: The Angular frontend follows vertical slice architecture matching the API project. Features are organized under `modules/` with a single domain: `admin/` (admin console — OIDC-protected). Each feature slice lives in its own folder with `-list`/`-capture` component naming, **colocated with its own service, models, and any feature-specific utilities**. Only truly cross-cutting services (auth, error handler) live in `core/`. Reusable UI lives in `shared/`.

examples.name: Project Structure Example 1
examples.code:
Nxgn.Actions.Web.Ui/
│
├── package.json                  # @nxgn-solutions/ui, AG Grid, Bootstrap 5, ngx-translate, ngx-toastr
├── angular.json                  # Application builder, SCSS, budgets
├── tsconfig.json                 # Path aliases: @app/, @core/, @shared/, @env
├── proxy.conf.js                 # Aspire service discovery → API proxy
│
├── public/                       # Static assets (favicon, etc.)
│
└── src/
    ├── main.ts                   # AG Grid module registration + bootstrap
    ├── index.html                # Outfit font, actions-branded title
    ├── _design-tokens.scss       # --actions-* CSS custom properties
    ├── styles.scss               # Bootstrap overrides + token imports
    │
    ├── environments/
    │   ├── environment.ts        # { production: false, apiBaseUrl: '/api' }
    │   └── environment.prod.ts   # { production: true, apiBaseUrl: '/api' }
    │
    └── app/
        ├── app.ts                # Root component — <router-outlet /> only
        ├── app.config.ts         # Providers: router, HTTP client, error handler
        ├── app.routes.ts         # Route definitions with lazy loading
        │
        ├── core/                 # Cross-cutting singletons
        ├── shared/               # Reusable components, pipes, utils
        └── modules/              # Feature slices (vertical)
            └── admin/            # Admin console pages (OIDC-protected)

examples.name: Feature Organization Example 1

examples.name: Core Layer (`core/`) Example 1
examples.code:
core/
├── guards/
│   └── admin-auth.guard.ts            # CanActivateFn — OIDC PKCE for /admin/*
├── interceptors/
│   └── auth.interceptor.ts            # HttpInterceptorFn — token attachment
├── models/
│   └── auth.models.ts                 # Shared interfaces (UserInfo, SessionContext)
└── services/
    ├── auth.service.ts                # Auth state, login/logout, token refresh

examples.name: Reusable components with zero feature dependencies. Used across admin modules.
examples.code:
shared/
├── components/
│   └── page-header/
│       ├── page-header.component.ts   # Standalone, input() for title/subtitle
│       └── index.ts                   # Barrel export
├── pipes/
│   └── errors-for.pipe.ts            # fieldErrors | errorsFor:'fieldName'
└── utils/
    └── api-error.util.ts             # parseApiError(), applyApiError()

examples.name: When fully implemented
examples.code:
modules/admin/actions/
├── action.service.ts              # HTTP calls — owns the API surface
├── action.models.ts               # Interfaces/DTOs — owns the data shapes
├── action-list.component.ts       # Grid: injects ActionService
├── action-list.component.html     # Template (extract from inline when complex)
├── action-capture.component.ts    # Form: injects ActionService
├── action-capture.component.html  # Form template
└── action-list.component.spec.ts  # Tests

examples.name: This mirrors the API's vertical slice
examples.code:
Features/Actions/
├── Shared/
│   ├── Queries/GetActionQuery.cs          ← action.service.ts (reads)
│   ├── Commands/SaveActionCommand.cs      ← action.service.ts (writes)
│   ├── Mappers/ActionDTOMapper.cs         ← (server-side only)
│   └── ActionDTO.cs                       ← action.models.ts
├── Get/GetActionEndpoint.cs               ← action-list → action-capture
├── Save/SaveActionEndpoint.cs             ← action-capture (create/edit)
└── List/ListActionsEndpoint.cs            ← action-list grid data

examples.name: File Placement Decision Tree Example 1
examples.code:
I need to add...
│
├─► An HTTP service for a feature → modules/admin/{feature}/{entity}.service.ts
│   Colocated with the feature. Owns the API surface for this entity.
│   Components inject this service — never raw HttpClient.
│
├─► Feature-local models/DTOs → modules/admin/{feature}/{entity}.models.ts
│   Colocated with the feature. Request/response shapes for the service.
│
├─► An admin list page → modules/admin/{feature}/{entity}-list.component.ts
│   Protected by adminAuthGuard. Uses nxgn-grid-page-title + nxgn-data-grid.
│
├─► A capture/form page → modules/admin/{feature}/{entity}-capture.component.ts
│   Handles new + edit. Route: /entity/new and /entity/:id.
│
├─► A capture state service → modules/admin/{feature}/{entity}-capture.state.ts
│   ONLY when capture page has child routes. Provided at component level.
│
├─► An embedded child list → modules/admin/{feature}/{parent}-{child}-list.component.ts
│   Uses model() for two-way binding with parent capture page.
│
├─► A reusable component → shared/components/{component-name}/
│   Must have zero feature dependencies. Used across admin.
│
├─► A cross-cutting service → core/services/
│   ONLY if used by 2+ features. Auth, error handling, etc.
│   Ask: "Would this still be needed if I deleted the feature folder?"
│
├─► A route guard → core/guards/
│   Functional CanActivateFn.
│
├─► An HTTP interceptor → core/interceptors/
│   Functional HttpInterceptorFn.
│
└─► Shared interfaces/types → core/models/
    Types used across multiple features (UserInfo, GridListResponse, etc.).

examples.name: Configuration change in app.config.ts
Before (Promise-based service method)
After (httpResource)

rules.rule_text: **Colocate services with features** — `actions/action.service.ts`, not `core/services/action.service.ts`
**One feature per folder** — `actions/action-list.component.ts`, not multiple features mixed
**Components inject feature services** — never raw `HttpClient` in components
**Standalone components** — no NgModules, ever
**OnPush change detection** — on every component
**Signal-based state** — `signal()` for local state, `computed()` for derived
**Functional DI** — `inject()`, not constructor parameters
**Lazy loading** - feature route groups use `loadChildren` returning standalone `Routes` arrays; leaf routes use `loadComponent`
**All user-facing strings through `ngx-translate`** — `translate` pipe in templates, `TranslateService.get()` in code. No hardcoded English strings.
**Page-level feedback via `ToastrService`** — success/error after save, domain commands. Inline alerts for validation and in-process indicators only.
**Start with inline templates** — extract to `.html` file when the template exceeds ~20 lines
**Use `@nxgn-solutions/ui` components** - `nxgn-server-grid` for standalone lists, `nxgn-data-grid` for embedded capture child rows, NxGN form fields for capture pages
**Use `applyApiError()`** — consistent error display across all forms
**Follow completed Actions features as templates** — copy a working feature slice
**Put feature services in `core/services/`** — only auth, error handler, and truly cross-cutting services belong there
**Import from other feature folders** — use shared/ or core/ for cross-feature needs
**Use raw `HttpClient` in components** — delegate to the colocated feature service
**Put business logic in components** — delegate to services or keep in the API
**Use NgModules** — standalone only
**Use BehaviorSubject for local state** — use signals

sections.title: Project Structure
content: ├── package.json                  # @nxgn-solutions/ui, AG Grid, Bootstrap 5, ngx-translate, ngx-toastr
├── proxy.conf.js                 # Aspire service discovery → API proxy
├── main.ts                   # AG Grid module registration + bootstrap
├── app.config.ts         # Providers: router, HTTP client, error handler
├── app.routes.ts         # Route definitions with lazy loading

sections.title: Feature Organization
content: OIDC-protected pages for platform administration. All routes under `/admin/*` are guarded by `adminAuthGuard`. Each feature is a **self-contained vertical slice** with its own components, service, and models colocated together.

sections.title: Core Layer (`core/`)
content: **Only truly cross-cutting concerns** that span multiple features. If a service is only used by one feature, it belongs in that feature folder — not here.
├── auth.service.ts                # Auth state, login/logout, token refresh
**Litmus test:** if you delete a feature folder, would this service still be needed by other features? If yes → `core/`. If no → colocate it with the feature.

sections.title: Shared Layer (`shared/`)
content: Reusable components with **zero feature dependencies**. Used across admin modules.

sections.title: Component Conventions
content: | **Signal state** | `signal()` for local state, not BehaviorSubject |
### Anatomy of a Complete Feature Slice
├── action.service.ts              # HTTP calls — owns the API surface
├── action.models.ts               # Interfaces/DTOs — owns the data shapes
├── action-list.component.html     # Template (extract from inline when complex)
This mirrors the API's vertical slice:

sections.title: Naming Conventions
content: **No `Admin` prefix** on class names — the folder path provides context.

sections.title: File Placement Decision Tree
content: ├─► An HTTP service for a feature → modules/admin/{feature}/{entity}.service.ts
│   Colocated with the feature. Owns the API surface for this entity.
│   Components inject this service — never raw HttpClient.
│   Colocated with the feature. Request/response shapes for the service.
│   Protected by adminAuthGuard. Uses nxgn-grid-page-title + nxgn-data-grid.
│   ONLY when capture page has child routes. Provided at component level.
│   Uses model() for two-way binding with parent capture page.
│   Must have zero feature dependencies. Used across admin.
│   ONLY if used by 2+ features. Auth, error handling, etc.
│   Ask: "Would this still be needed if I deleted the feature folder?"
Types used across multiple features (UserInfo, GridListResponse, etc.).

sections.title: Required Packages
content: These packages are **required** infrastructure for all web patterns. If they are not yet installed, install them before implementing any new feature.
| `@ngx-translate/core` | i18n — all user-facing strings | All components (templates via `translate` pipe, code via `TranslateService`) |
| `ngx-toastr` | Page-level notifications (save success, errors) | Capture pages, list pages, domain commands |
| `@nxgn-solutions/ui` | Shared UI components (grid, form fields, page titles) | All pages |
| `@ag-grid-community/angular` | Data grid | List pages, embedded lists |
| `@ng-bootstrap/ng-bootstrap` | Modals, tooltips, nav tabs | Capture pages (modals), tabbed interfaces |
When adding `ngx-translate` and `ngx-toastr`, register them in `app.config.ts`:
provideAnimationsAsync(),  // Required by ngx-toastr and ng-bootstrap (async = non-blocking)

A translation loader service must be implemented in `core/services/translations/` to fetch translations from the API (matching Capstone's pattern). This is a cross-cutting service — it belongs in `core/`, not a feature folder.
├── http-translation-loader.ts    # Implements TranslateLoader, fetches from API

sections.title: Rules
content: 2. **One feature per folder** - `actions/action-list.component.ts`, not multiple features mixed
3. **Components inject feature services** - never raw `HttpClient` in components
6. **Signal-based state** - `signal()` for local state, `computed()` for derived
8. **Lazy loading** - feature route groups use `loadChildren` returning standalone `Routes` arrays; leaf routes use `loadComponent`
9. **All user-facing strings through `ngx-translate`** - `translate` pipe in templates, `TranslateService.get()` or `instant()` in code. No hardcoded English strings.
10. **Page-level feedback via `ToastrService` or inline alerts** - success/error after save and domain commands. Inline alerts for validation, degraded grid state, and in-process indicators.
1. **Start with inline templates** - extract to `.html` file when the template exceeds ~20 lines
2. **Use `@nxgn-solutions/ui` components** - `nxgn-server-grid` for standalone lists, `nxgn-data-grid` for embedded capture child rows, NxGN form fields for capture pages
3. **Use `applyApiError()` / `extractApiError()`** - consistent error display across all forms and list commands
4. **Follow completed Actions features as templates** - copy a working feature slice
1. **Put feature services in `core/services/`** - only auth, error handler, and truly cross-cutting services belong there
2. **Import from other feature folders** - use shared/ or core/ for cross-feature needs
3. **Use raw `HttpClient` in components** - delegate to the colocated feature service
4. **Put business logic in components** - delegate to services or keep in the API
6. **Use BehaviorSubject for local state** - use signals
7. **Use `nxgn-data-grid` for standalone admin list pages** - use `nxgn-server-grid` and a grid-list endpoint

sections.title: Target Pattern (ADR-0012, httpResource)
content: > **Status: Proposed.** This section describes target patterns for the Angular frontend.
> The current patterns above remain in effect until migration is complete.
Remove Zone.js and enable zoneless change detection. The project already uses signals and OnPush everywhere — this completes the transition.
**Component rules unchanged:** OnPush + signals is already the standard. Zoneless simply removes the Zone.js overhead — no component code changes needed.

Angular's `httpResource()` (available in Angular 19.2+) replaces `firstValueFrom(http.get())` for read operations. It returns a signal-based resource that integrates naturally with the signal-first architecture.
// In component — no service method needed for simple GETs
| `httpResource()` | Simple GET requests where the URL is derived from signals (route params, selections) |
| Feature service method | Complex requests with headers, params, or shared across components |
`httpResource()` returns a `ResourceRef` with `.value()`, `.isLoading()`, `.error()`, and `.reload()` — all signals. This eliminates manual loading state management for read operations.
